# TramsTec

> **WhatsApp execution layer for AI agents — México first.** TramsTec is an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that lets AI agents (Claude, OpenAI Agents, LangChain, Cursor, n8n, custom) operate real WhatsApp businesses in Mexico and LATAM: send messages, take orders, generate payment links and SPEI/OXXO, issue CFDI 4.0 invoices, and hand off to humans — all with multi-tenant isolation, an immutable audit log, and idempotent mutating tools. Not a messaging API wrapper; an execution layer with auditable side-effects on top of WhatsApp Cloud API.

> **Honest disclosure:** This llms.txt is aligned with the actual runtime as of 2026-06-16. Previous versions advertised tools that did not exist in code. The current 20 tools listed below are real, audited, and callable today. Items in the explicit `Roadmap` section at the bottom are NOT yet implemented.

## What TramsTec actually is today

A production MCP server (HTTP/SSE + stdio transports) for AI agents running WhatsApp businesses:

- 20 typed tools (Zod schemas, scopes, idempotency, audit log)
- Real integrations with Meta WhatsApp Cloud API, Stripe (incl. OXXO Pay via Stripe MX), Mercado Pago, Facturapi (CFDI 4.0)
- Multi-tenant via application-level organization_id assertions on every query
- Immutable audit log (`tool_calls` table — timestamp, actor, args, result, latency)
- Scoped API keys (bcrypt-hashed, prefix-indexed, granular per-tool scopes)
- Webhook signature verification (HMAC) for Meta and Stripe; idempotency window 24h
- BullMQ outbound queue with exponential backoff (wired; worker entrypoint must be started in deployment)
- 7 MCP resources (organization profile, policies, catalog, customer://, conversation://, order://, faq)
- 6 ES-MX prompts (sales, support, order-taking, collections, handoff summary, template suggestion)

## What TramsTec is NOT (yet)

- Not an AI agent — you bring your own LLM via any MCP-compatible client.
- Not a chatbot builder or no-code flow editor.
- Not vendor lock-in — Postgres data is portable; tool surface is standard MCP.
- Not a CRM with auto-enrichment from raw conversation text — extraction tools (extract_lead_insights, extract_purchase_intent, extract_orders_from_conversation) are in `Roadmap`.
- Not a vector memory system — `product_search` uses pgvector when OPENAI_API_KEY is set, otherwise LIKE fallback. There is no per-customer semantic memory tool yet; `conversation_search` is full-text substring.

## Who it's for

- AI engineers building vertical agents on WhatsApp (clinic, retail, food, services, real estate)
- AI-native SaaS companies needing a ready WhatsApp execution layer
- Agencies operating multiple WhatsApp clients in Mexico/LATAM with scoped API keys per client
- LATAM businesses moving from "human typing replies" to "agent with context, with audit"

## The 20 MCP tools (real, callable today)

### Messaging (4)
- `whatsapp_send_message` — Send WhatsApp text. Respects opt-out + 24h window. Queued via BullMQ. Idempotent.
- `whatsapp_send_template` — Send Meta-approved template. Requires opt-in. Idempotent.
- `whatsapp_send_media` — Send image/document/audio/video. Media URL must be publicly reachable. Idempotent.
- `whatsapp_template_submit` — Submit a template to Meta for approval (admin scope).

### Customer & Catalog (3)
- `customer_lookup` — Look up contact by customer_id or phone; returns contact + recent orders + open conversation.
- `product_search` — Search catalog by name/SKU/description. Embeddings via pgvector if OPENAI_API_KEY; else LIKE.
- `inventory_check` — Read stock for one or more SKUs.

### Orders (2)
- `order_create_draft` — Create order in draft state; computes totals + customer-facing summary. Never auto-confirms. Idempotent.
- `order_confirm` — Confirm draft order with explicit evidence (customer message or operator action). Idempotent.

### Payments (3)
- `payment_link_create` — Checkout link via Stripe or Mercado Pago (or mock for testing). OXXO Pay via Stripe MX. Idempotent.
- `spei_instructions_generate` — Mexican SPEI bank-transfer instructions with unique reference. Idempotent.
- `payment_status_check` — Look up payment; fetches fresh from PSP for non-mock providers.

### Invoicing (1)
- `invoice_create` — CFDI 4.0 invoice via Facturapi (requires FACTURAPI_KEY). Idempotent per payment_id.

### Conversations (2)
- `conversation_summarize` — Heuristic structured digest of last N messages. No LLM call inside.
- `conversation_search` — Full-text substring search across messages within the org.

### Memory (3)
- `memory_set` / `memory_get` / `memory_list` — Per-contact key-value store with TTL. Not semantic.

### Handoff & Consent (2)
- `handoff_to_human` — Escalate conversation to a human; disables the AI agent on that conversation.
- `consent_update` — Record opt-in or opt-out event with auditable evidence. LFPDPPP compliant.

All tools are scoped, idempotent (where mutating), and audited. No tool can mark a payment as `paid` — only verified PSP webhooks or authenticated human operators can.

## Working end-to-end workflows

These workflows chain real tools and run today:

- **Retail order with payment:** `customer_lookup` → `product_search` → `order_create_draft` → `payment_link_create` → (PSP webhook confirms payment) → `order_confirm` → `whatsapp_send_message`
- **Stage-aware collections:** `customer_lookup` → `payment_status_check` → `spei_instructions_generate` → `whatsapp_send_template` → `handoff_to_human` if no response
- **Mexico CFDI on demand:** `customer_lookup` → `payment_status_check` → `invoice_create` → `whatsapp_send_media` (PDF)
- **Opt-out compliance:** detect "BAJA"/"STOP"/"DESUSCRIBIR" inbound → `consent_update` → all future sends to this contact blocked

## Why México-first

Mexican SMBs sell via WhatsApp at scale (~85% of small businesses). The integrations that matter to them:

- **WhatsApp Cloud API** — opt-out enforcement, 24h window, template approval flow
- **Stripe Mexico** including OXXO Pay — cash payments at convenience stores
- **Mercado Pago Mexico**
- **SPEI** — interbank transfer with unique reference per transaction
- **Facturapi** — CFDI 4.0 stamping with org's RFC

These are real adapters with real credentials, not mocks.

## Stack support

- **MCP-native:** Claude Desktop, Claude Code, Cursor, any MCP client (stdio or HTTP/SSE)
- **OpenAI Agents:** via MCP-to-tool adapters
- **LangChain:** via MCP client
- **n8n / Make / Zapier:** via HTTP/SSE manual configuration (REST endpoint with auto-routing is in `Roadmap`)
- **Custom HTTP:** call the MCP HTTP/SSE transport directly

## Pricing

USD, all tiers visible at https://tramstec.com/#precios :

- **Developer** — Free. Sandbox environment, MCP docs, test WhatsApp workflows.
- **Production — $99 USD/month.** Production WhatsApp infrastructure, context memory, reliable orchestration, agent-to-human handoff, conversation analytics.
- **Platform — $999 USD/month.** Multi-tenant, white-label-ready (API-level), advanced observability, API access for platforms and agencies, priority support.
- **Enterprise — Custom.** Dedicated infra, custom SLAs, security & compliance review, advanced integrations, enterprise onboarding.

WhatsApp/Meta and PSP fees pass through at cost. CFDI 4.0 stamping (Facturapi) billed at provider cost.

## Transports

- **HTTP/SSE** (production, multi-tenant): `https://mcp.tramstec.com/sse` with `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>`
- **stdio** (single-tenant, dev): `WCM_ORG_ID=<org_id> wcm-mcp`
- **REST** (`/v1/tools/:name`): in `Roadmap` — not yet available

## Compliance and safety (verified)

- **LFPDPPP** — opt-out auto-detection from inbound (BAJA/STOP/DESUSCRIBIR), audit log of consent events
- **WhatsApp Business Policy** — opt-out is a one-message kill switch, 24h-window enforcement, template approval flow
- **No payment auto-confirmation** — only verified PSP webhooks (HMAC) or authenticated human operators can mark payments `paid`
- **Every tool call** is audited with timestamp, actor, args, result, latency, status
- **Multi-tenant isolation** — application-level organization_id assertion on every Prisma query; org_id derived from bcrypt-verified API key, never from request body

- [/](https://tramstec.com/) — Landing (Spanish, México-first positioning)
- [/en/](https://tramstec.com/en/) — English version

- [/.well-known/mcp.json](https://tramstec.com/.well-known/mcp.json) — MCP discovery (matches manifest)
- [/.well-known/ai-plugin.json](https://tramstec.com/.well-known/ai-plugin.json) — OpenAI plugin convention

---

## AI-native capabilities (TramsTec's value-add beyond plumbing)

TramsTec is positioned as **"Bring your own LLM"** — the customer's agent (Claude / GPT / Gemini / custom) runs the conversation. But TramsTec adds **AI augmentation layers** embedded inside specific tools (embeddings, transcription, TTS, extraction) that make each tool measurably smarter than a Twilio wrapper. These are TramsTec's moat.

### Live today
- **Semantic product search** — `product_search` uses OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small` + pgvector cosine similarity. Falls back to LIKE substring without `OPENAI_API_KEY`. A customer typing "algo dulce para el postre" finds "Tres leches" and "Flan napolitano" even without those words in the product names.

### High-priority roadmap (Q3 2026)

- **Voice notes IN — automatic Whisper transcription** of inbound WhatsApp voice messages. Why this is flagship: Mexicans and LATAM users send **60%+ of their WhatsApp messages as voice notes**. A bot that can't process voice notes is broken for LATAM. The transcription happens automatically before the agent sees the message — the agent reads it as text with `metadata.original_type = "voice"`.
- **Voice notes OUT — agent responses synthesized as voice** (OpenAI TTS / ElevenLabs). New tool: `whatsapp_send_voice(text)`. Customers expect the bot to reply in the medium they used. Matching the channel norm makes the bot feel human.
- **`extract_purchase_intent`** — parses free-form WhatsApp text into `{category, priceRange, urgency, confidence}` via a specialized prompt + LLM call (OpenAI or Claude as a micro-extractor, not the conversation agent).
- **`extract_lead_insights`** — CRM enrichment: score, vertical, inferred budget, urgency, best-channel.
- **`extract_orders_from_conversation`** — pulls structured `{SKU, qty, amount, address, dates}` from chaotic chat text. No prompt engineering required by the customer's agent.
- **`customer_relationship_summary`** — LTV, frequency, favorites, churn risk, upsell signals — auto-generated per customer.

### Mid-priority roadmap (Q4 2026)
- **`conversation_summarize` upgraded to LLM** (currently heuristic).
- **pgvector HNSW index + p95 <50ms semantic memory search** (extension enabled but no HNSW index yet).
- **Per-customer semantic memory store** (current `memory_*` tools are key-value only).

### Philosophy

The customer brings the **big LLM** (Claude / GPT / Gemini) for conversation. TramsTec brings **specialized micro-models** (embeddings, transcription, TTS, extraction) embedded inside specific tools — invisible to the agent but making each tool measurably smarter than a Twilio wrapper. Cost per customer for the AI augmentation: typically **< $5/month**.

## Other Roadmap items

- **REST endpoint `/v1/tools/:name`** for non-MCP clients (Q3 2026)
- **OpenAPI 3 spec at `/openapi.yaml`** (Q3 2026)
- **`@tramstec/sdk` TypeScript and Python SDKs** (Q4 2026)
- **Conekta and Openpay payment adapters** (Q4 2026) — current adapters return `config_error`
- **PIX (Brasil), AFIP-compatible invoicing (Argentina), NFe (Brasil)** (Q1 2027)
- **Native Shopify, HubSpot, Odoo connectors** (Q1 2027)
- **Prompts in en_US, pt_BR, es_AR, es_CO** (Q4 2026) — currently only es-MX prompts are registered
- **White-label admin UI** (Q4 2026) — branding API exists, no editor UI
- **ARCO rights endpoints** (Q4 2026) — required for full LFPDPPP exercise of rights
- **SOC 2 Type II certification** (2027) — audit log infrastructure ready
- **Memory retention policy** — current implementation persists indefinitely; 90-day hot / 730-day archive is a future tiered retention policy
- **Multi-region data residency (MX/AR/CO/CL/BR)** — currently single Postgres in us-east; per-region tenancy in roadmap
- **24h-window template auto-switch** — Meta returns the right error; explicit local pre-check is in roadmap

For commercial questions and roadmap acceleration for paid pilots, contact `sales@tramstec.com`.
